themes.label: Kingdom
themes.summary: This chapter stresses reign, kingdom, throne, rule, or the authority of God and His Christ. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 12 verses. Commentary leans on "Then I came to the governors beyond the river, and gave them the king's letters. Now the king...". Cross references echo "Kingdom".

themes.label: Presence
themes.summary: This chapter highlights God’s nearness, abiding presence, indwelling, or covenant companionship. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 4 verses. Commentary leans on "And I arose in the night, I and some few men with me; neither told I any man what my God had...". Word-study detail reinforces "אֶל: - near, with or among; often in general, to".

themes.label: Peace/Rest
themes.summary: This chapter offers peace, rest, stillness, comfort, or settled safety in God. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 1 verse. Commentary leans on "And the rulers knew not whither I went, or what I did; neither had I as yet told it to the Je...". Word-study detail reinforces "וּלְיֶ֙תֶר֙: nor to the rest - properly, an overhanging, i.e., (by implication) a small rope...".

themes.label: Provision
themes.summary: This chapter emphasizes God’s supplying care, nourishment, sufficiency, or generous provision. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 1 verse. Commentary leans on "Then answered I them, and said unto them, The God of heaven, he will prosper us; therefore we...". Word-study detail reinforces "חֵ֧לֶק: but ye have no portion - properly, smoothness (of the tongue)".
